What'd I Miss?

Raised in the Bay and shaped on the Central Coast, I've learned to be comfortable not having everything figured out. Three main principles guide my life: be intentional, stay present, and take the next step—even when the path isn't clear.

Outside of academics, you'll usually find me playing sports, learning more about nutrition, or volunteering in my community. At Cal Poly, I'm finishing up my second year studying accounting and finance, where I approach my work with the same energy and curiosity.

California Polytechnic State University, SLO

Serving as a vital link between university housing administration and the student body. Managing high-volume residential services for a large student population—ensuring essential needs from facility access to move-out procedures are handled with speed and precision. Acting as an organizational liaison, translating complex policy updates and community concerns into transparent communication for residents.

Community Action Partnership San Luis Obispo

Working as a Payroll and HR Assistant, ensuring the financial accuracy and regulatory health of a major community organization. Reconciling payroll accounts and internal financial statements daily. Maintaining strict federal and state compliance through detailed audits for the U.S. Department of Labor and the California EDD.

Prototype Vehicles Laboratory

Cal Poly Global

Selected as 1 of 22 students for an intensive six-week immersion program exploring business landscapes of Japan and South Korea. Analyzed intersections of East Asian technology, cultural dynamics, and corporate practices. Continuing as a program representative providing guidance to future cohorts.
